title: GoT-ChA: Genotyping of Targeted loci with single-cell Chromatin Accessibility
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"9nj9c","text":"Somatic mutations are crucial for cancer initiation and evolution, and have been identified across a number of healthy tissues in the human body. These mutations can disrupt normal cellular functions, leading to aberrant clonal expansions via acquired fitness advantages or skewed differentiation topologies. GoT-ChA and similar methods (e.g. GoT, TARGET-seq) aim to pair targeted genotyping with single-cell sequencing approaches in order to understand the impact of somatic mutations directly in human patient samples, in both malignant and non-malignant contexts.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"50pdq","text":"GoT-ChA is unique in that it is a high-throughput single-cell method that pairs targeted genotyping with chromatin accessibility measurements based on the broadly utilized scATAC-seq platform from 10x Genomics. Previous single-cell genotyping approaches were largely based on scRNA-seq protocols, utilizing expressed and captured mRNA transcripts as sources for genotyping information. This results in a limiting dependence on gene expression and mutation location (due to 3' end bias of most scRNA-seq methods), precluding the usage of such technologies on lowly expressed or inconveniently located loci of interest. GoT-ChA surmounts these limitations via direct utilization of genomic DNA for genotyping, whilst simultaneously assaying chromatin accessibility.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"3tooc","text":"How does GoT-ChA work?","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":22}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"chfgi","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4u8ql","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"7ep5r","text":"In order to capture genotypes within droplet-based scATAC-seq, two GoT-ChA primers are added into the cell barcoding PCR reaction that are designed to flank the locus of interest. One primer contains the partial Nextera Read 1N sequence in its handle, which allows for GoT-ChA amplicons to interact with the 10x Genomics gel bead oligonucleotide and obtain a unique cell barcode, just as tagmented chromatin fragments do. Further, the second GoT-ChA primer allows for exponential amplification of GoT-ChA amplicons while tagmented chromatin fragments are only linearly amplified.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5jt51","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bfu76","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"fh663","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ejv1d","text":"After the single-cell emulsion is broken, a small portion of the sample is taken for GoT-ChA library construction, comprised of a hemi-nested PCR, biotin-streptavidin pull-down, and an on-bead sample indexing PCR. The final GoT-ChA library can be pooled with scATAC-seq libraries and sequenced together using standard ATAC sequencing parameters.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"image","mutability":"IMMUTABLE","data":{"align":"","alt":"","guid":"B171C481829311EF84B30A58A9FEAC02","height":109.67272727272751,"id":504023,"is_video":false,"legend":"{\"blocks\":null,\"entityMap\":null}","mime":"image/png","nestedSelector":null,"original_name":"HowWorks1.png","placeholder":"https://content.protocols.io/files/rm4ychwff.png","shadow":false,"source":"https://content.protocols.io/files/rngzchwff.jpg","webp_source":"https://content.protocols.io/files/rngychwff.webp","width":580}},"1":{"type":"image","mutability":"IMMUTABLE","data":{"alt":"","guid":"B6E1B251829311EF84B30A58A9FEAC02","height":121.2727272727273,"id":504025,"is_video":false,"legend":"{\"blocks\":null,\"entityMap\":null}","mime":"image/png","nestedSelector":null,"original_name":"HowWorks2.png","placeholder":"https://content.protocols.io/files/rm43chwff.png","shadow":false,"source":"https://content.protocols.io/files/rng3chwff.jpg","webp_source":"https://content.protocols.io/files/rng2chwff.webp","width":580}}}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"7nh3m","text":"Designing a GoT-ChA experiment.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":31}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ae0ih","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"epu3g","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8unu7","text":"To utilize GoT-ChA, three primers need to be designed that flank the genomic region of interest. GoT-ChA_R1 (containing the partial Nextera Read1 sequence in its handle) and GoT-ChA_Rev flank the loci of interest, ideally forming an amplicon between 200-500 bps in size. GoT-ChA_nested is utilized in the hemi-nested PCR during GoT-ChA library construction, and crucially needs to bind within 50bp (inclusive of binding site) of the mutation to be covered with standard scATAC-seq sequencing parameters.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"image","mutability":"IMMUTABLE","data":{"alt":"","guid":"A58985A9829311EF84B30A58A9FEAC02","height":90.69090909090907,"id":504017,"is_video":false,"legend":"{\"blocks\":null,\"entityMap\":null}","mime":"image/png","nestedSelector":null,"original_name":"DesignOligos.png","placeholder":"https://content.protocols.io/files/rm47chwff.png","shadow":false,"source":"https://content.protocols.io/files/rngtchwff.jpg","webp_source":"https://content.protocols.io/files/rngschwff.webp","width":580}}}}
steps: Pre-chill swing-bucket centrifuge to 4˚C

Obtain all single cell suspensions (filter if needed) and measure viability and density (if viability is <90% proceed with live cell enrichment and/or use best judgement depending on sample source / importance / rarity).
Pre-heat block to 65˚C for later use dissolving digitonin for GoT-ChA on Isolated Nuclei

Wash buffer A B C D E 1 reagent stock final µL for 1 mL µL for 10 mL 2 Tris-HCl (pH 7.4) 1 M 10 mM 10 100 3 NaCl 5 M 10 mM 2 20 4 MgCl2 1 M 3 mM 3 30 5 BSA 10% 1% 100 1000 6 Tween 20 (for Nuclei prep only) 10% 0.1% 10 100 7 Nuclease-free H20 875/865 8750/8650 prepare fresh, keep at 4˚C

Lysis Buffer A B C D E 1 reagent stock final µL for 1 mL (whole cell) µL for 1 mL (Nuclei) 2 Tris-HCl (pH 7.4) 1 M 10 mM 10 10 3 NaCl 5 M 10 mM 2 2 4 MgCl2 1 M 3 mM 3 3 5 NP-40 10% 0.1% 10 10 6 BSA 10% 1% 100 100 7 Tween20 10% 0.1% - 10ul 8 Digitonin 5% 0.01% - 2ul 9 Nucelase-free H2O 875 863 prepare fresh, keep at 4˚C
PBS + 0.04% BSA A B C D 1 reagent stock final µL for 1 mL 2 BSA 10% 0.04% 4 3 PBS 996
Filter through 40 μm strainers
Remove supernatant, resuspend in 150 μl 1x nuclei buffer (10x Genomics)
Spin 5 minutes 500g at 4 ̊C.
Add 1 ml chilled wash buffer to the lysed cells, mix by pipetting
Incubate on ice for 3min (primary cells), 5min (cell lines)
Resuspended cell pellet in 100 μl chilled lysis buffer, mix by pipetting.
Spin 5 minutes 400g at 4 ̊C, discard supernatant.
Discard supernatant and repeat wash with 1ml 1x ice-cold PBS
Spin 5 minutes 400g at 4 ̊C.
Wash with 1x ice-cold PBS by filling up the tube, invert 5 times
Quench by adding glycine to 0.125M f.c.
Add 30 μl 16% formaldehyde (1% f.c) and incubate 10min at room temperature, swirl, invert occasionally
Resuspend cells in 450 μl PBS. if low cell number, scale down accordingly.

Obtain all single cell suspensions (filter if needed) and measure viability and density (if viability is <90% proceed with live cell enrichment and/or use best judgement depending on sample source / importance / rarity).

Carry with Transposition for sc-experiment using 10x kit and follow the protocol. (remember to spike in 1µL of combined Forward and Reverse (each at 22.5µM conc) to the emulsion Master Mix) corresponding to step 2.1 from 10X genomics ATAC user guide
Calculate amount of µL for target number of recovered cells (max volume for loading is 5 µL for 10x transposition reaction)
Use 2 µL nuclei suspension mixed with 6 µL Diluted Nuclei Buffer and 2 µL trypan blue to determine nuclei concentration
Resuspend the pellet in X µL of chilled Diluted Nuclei Buffer for a final volume close to 7-8 µL
Remove supernatant in two steps to not touch pellet
Spin 5 minutes at 700 x g at 4 ˚C
Add 1 ml chilled wash buffer to the lysed cells and mix via pipetting
Incubate on ice for 3 minutes (primary cells) or 5 minutes (cell lines)
Resuspend cell pellet in 100 µL chilled lysis buffer and mix via pipetting
Remove supernatant and resuspend in 100 µL room temperature PBS + BSA if low number of cells, scale done volume
As per 10x protocol. (remember to spike in Gotcha primers (22.5uM - 1ul each) per reaction during emulsion generation step) Step 2.5 from the 10X Genomics ATAC User guide
Continue with SPRI clean up and elute DNA with 44.5ul of EB buffer. (40ul for ATAC and 4ul for GOTCHA) Step 3.2 from the 10X Genomics ATAC User guide
Proceed to ATAC-library process as per 10x protocol with 40ul of eluted DNA.
Perform 10-20 cycles of PCR with the following conditions (with nested primer)
GoTChA Nested PCR thermocycler program A B C 1 1 cycle 3 min 95˚C 2 10-20 cycles 20 sec 95˚C 3 30 sec 65˚C 4 1 min 72˚C 5 1 cycle 2 min 72˚C 6 hold 4˚C
GoTChA Nested PCR A B 1 KAPA reagents 1X (in µL) 2 DNA input 20 ( 4 DNA+16 Water) 3 p5 generic (10 µM) 2.5 4 GoTChA nested primer (10 µM) Biotiylated 2.5 5 KAPA (2X) 25 6 total volume: 50
SPRI clean up with 1.2X. Elute in 40 µL.
Allocate 15 µL of streptavidin M-280 beads per sample (+10%)
Resuspend beads in 20 µL
Wash one time with 100ul of 10 mM Tris HCl (pH 8.0)
Wash two times with 100ul of 1x SSPE buffer
Incubate at room temperature for 15 minutes
Add 10 µL of beads to 40 µL of SPRI'd sample
Resuspend in 5X SSPE buffer for 10 µL per sample (+10%)
Wash beads three times with 100ul of 1x SSPE buffer
Perform 6-10 cycles of PCR with the following conditions:
GoTChA Sample Index PCR A B 1 KAPA reagents 1X (in µL) 2 DNA input 20 3 p5 generic (10 µM) 2.5 4 RPI-X (10 µM) 2.5 5 KAPA (2X) 25 6 amt to add: 30 7 total volume: 50
GoTChA Sample Index thermocycler program A B C 1 1 cycle 3 min 95˚C 2 10-20 cycles 20 sec 95˚C 3 30 sec 65˚C 4 1 min 72˚C 5 1 cycle 2 min 72˚C 6 hold 4˚C
Run an e-gel to assess correct product formation. Use 5 µL (10%) of total sample
Purify libraries using AMPureXP beads at a 1.2X ratio
Elute in 20 µL of EB or Water
Run a BioA Expected results: Representative bioanalyzer traces of GoT-ChA genotyping (left) and GoT-ChA scATAC (rigth) libraries. FU, fluorescent units.

Dilute samples for sequencing. GoT-ChA libraries can be sequenced together with ATAC libraries assuming i7 indexes do not clash. Sequencing parameters are as follows Read 1N - 50 cycles, i7 index -8 cycles, i5 Index - 16 cycles. Read 2N - 50 cycles.
Measure samples via Qubit

Carry with Transposition for sc-experiment using 10x kit and follow the protocol. (remember to spike in 1µL of combined Forward and Reverse (each at 22.5µM conc) to the emulsion Master Mix) corresponding to step 2.1 from 10X genomics ATAC user guide
Spin down at 300 x g for 5 minutes at 4˚C in PCR tubes if the number of cells are very low.
Use 2 µL nuclei suspension mixed with 6 µL Diluted Nuclei Buffer and 2 µL trypan blue to determine nuclei concentration
Calculate amount of µL for target number of recovered cells (max volume for loading is 5 µL for 10x transposition reaction)
Spin down at 300 x g for 5 minutes at 4˚C
creator: Ivan Raimondi
